requiredExam.label: Auscultate heart rhythm
requiredExam.action: Document irregular rhythm because atrial fibrillation or other arrhythmia changes ECG interpretation, monitoring, and management.
requiredExam.technique: Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, murmurs, rubs, or extra heart sounds.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: arrhythmia, structural heart disease, ischemic complication, or heart-failure finding
requiredExam.management_change: Document irregular rhythm because atrial fibrillation or other arrhythmia changes ECG interpretation, monitoring, and management.
requiredExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

requiredExam.label: Auscultate for cardiac murmurs
requiredExam.action: Document a new murmur because it changes concern for valvular disease, mechanical complication, or aortic pathology.
requiredExam.technique: Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, murmurs, rubs, or extra heart sounds.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: arrhythmia, structural heart disease, ischemic complication, or heart-failure finding
requiredExam.management_change: Document a new murmur because it changes concern for valvular disease, mechanical complication, or aortic pathology.
requiredExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

requiredExam.label: Auscultate for gallop
requiredExam.action: Document an S3 or S4 when present because it changes heart failure or ischemic risk framing.
requiredExam.technique: Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, murmurs, rubs, or extra heart sounds.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: arrhythmia, structural heart disease, ischemic complication, or heart-failure finding
requiredExam.management_change: Document an S3 or S4 when present because it changes heart failure or ischemic risk framing.
requiredExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

requiredExam.label: Observe work of breathing
requiredExam.action: Document increased work of breathing because it changes respiratory support needs and cardiopulmonary emergency concern.
requiredExam.technique: Observe respiratory rate, depth, accessory muscle use, speech tolerance, and breathing pattern at rest.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: respiratory distress, acidosis compensation, pulmonary infection, obstruction, congestion, or pneumothorax pattern
requiredExam.management_change: Document increased work of breathing because it changes respiratory support needs and cardiopulmonary emergency concern.
requiredExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

requiredExam.label: Auscultate lung sounds
requiredExam.action: Document wheeze, crackles, or asymmetric breath sounds because these change heart failure, obstructive lung disease, pneumonia, or pneumothorax concern.
requiredExam.technique: Auscultate symmetric anterior, lateral, and posterior lung fields as feasible, comparing sides for crackles, wheeze, diminished sounds, or asymmetry.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: respiratory distress, acidosis compensation, pulmonary infection, obstruction, congestion, or pneumothorax pattern
requiredExam.management_change: Document wheeze, crackles, or asymmetric breath sounds because these change heart failure, obstructive lung disease, pneumonia, or pneumothorax concern.
requiredExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

requiredExam.label: Palpate radial pulses
requiredExam.action: Document diminished or asymmetric pulses because this changes concern for shock or acute aortic syndrome.
requiredExam.technique: Palpate bilateral pulses at the named sites, comparing amplitude, symmetry, rhythm, and perfusion.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: dehydration, shock physiology, or peripheral perfusion
requiredExam.management_change: Document diminished or asymmetric pulses because this changes concern for shock or acute aortic syndrome.
requiredExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

requiredExam.label: Inspect and palpate extremity perfusion
requiredExam.action: Document cool extremities, delayed capillary refill, or poor perfusion because these change shock assessment and escalation.
requiredExam.technique: Inspect and palpate distal extremities for warmth, color, capillary refill, mottling, and symmetry.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: dehydration, shock physiology, or peripheral perfusion
requiredExam.management_change: Document cool extremities, delayed capillary refill, or poor perfusion because these change shock assessment and escalation.
requiredExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

requiredExam.label: Measure blood pressure
requiredExam.action: Document hypotension, severe hypertension, or arm-to-arm differences because these change acuity, monitoring, and vascular emergency concern.
requiredExam.management_change: Document hypotension, severe hypertension, or arm-to-arm differences because these change acuity, monitoring, and vascular emergency concern.
requiredExam.limitations: Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context
requiredExam.technique: Measure blood pressure using an appropriately sized cuff after a brief rest period, seated or supine as clinically appropriate.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.

requiredExam.label: Measure heart rate
requiredExam.action: Document tachycardia, bradycardia, or irregularity because these change ECG urgency, monitoring, and cardiopulmonary differential.
requiredExam.management_change: Document tachycardia, bradycardia, or irregularity because these change ECG urgency, monitoring, and cardiopulmonary differential.
requiredExam.limitations: Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: tachycardia, bradycardia, irregular rhythm, or acute physiologic stress
requiredExam.technique: Palpate or monitor heart rate for a full interval, noting rate and regularity.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.

requiredExam.label: Measure respiratory rate
requiredExam.action: Document tachypnea or respiratory distress because this changes PE, pneumothorax, pneumonia, heart failure, and escalation concern.
requiredExam.management_change: Document tachypnea or respiratory distress because this changes PE, pneumothorax, pneumonia, heart failure, and escalation concern.
requiredExam.limitations: Respiratory rate is often undercounted and may be affected by anxiety, pain, fever, compensation for metabolic disease, or sedation; observe directly when it changes acuity.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: tachypnea, hypoventilation, respiratory compensation, or acute illness severity
requiredExam.technique: Count respiratory rate over a full interval, noting rate and effort.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.

requiredExam.label: Measure oxygen saturation
requiredExam.action: Document hypoxemia because it changes cardiopulmonary emergency triage and PE or pneumonia concern.
requiredExam.management_change: Document hypoxemia because it changes cardiopulmonary emergency triage and PE or pneumonia concern.
requiredExam.limitations: Pulse oximetry can be affected by poor perfusion, motion, nail products, dyshemoglobinemia, skin pigmentation, and device quality; interpret with work of breathing and clinical context.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: hypoxemia, oxygen requirement, respiratory support need, or cardiopulmonary acuity
requiredExam.technique: Measure oxygen saturation by pulse oximetry.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.

requiredExam.label: Observe general appearance
requiredExam.action: Document diaphoresis, distress, pallor, or toxic appearance because these change urgency and pretest concern for dangerous causes.
requiredExam.management_change: Document diaphoresis, distress, pallor, or toxic appearance because these change urgency and pretest concern for dangerous causes.
requiredExam.limitations: Global appearance and perfusion are subjective and affected by baseline function, pain, medications, temperature, and examiner experience; pair with objective vitals and reassessment.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: global acuity, shock/toxic appearance, distress, or immediate safety concern
requiredExam.technique: Observe general appearance, noting distress, color, and overall clinical impression.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.

requiredExam.label: Measure blood pressure in both arms
requiredExam.action: Add this when aortic syndrome concern is present because a major arm-to-arm difference changes urgency and imaging pathway.
requiredExam.management_change: Add this when aortic syndrome concern is present because a major arm-to-arm difference changes urgency and imaging pathway.
requiredExam.limitations: Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: inter-arm blood pressure asymmetry, severe hypertension, or aortic/dissection-risk bedside clue
requiredExam.technique: Measure blood pressure separately in both arms and compare for a clinically significant difference.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect jugular venous pressure
conditionalExam.action: Add this when dyspnea or heart failure features are present because elevated JVP changes congestion and right-heart strain assessment.
conditionalExam.technique: Position the patient at 30 to 45 degrees when possible and inspect the internal jugular venous pulsation, estimating height and waveform in context.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Add when dyspnea, orthopnea, edema, heart failure, volume overload or related patient-specific modifiers are present in the selected Chest pain workup.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: venous congestion, right-heart strain, or volume-sensitive cardiopulmonary disease
conditionalExam.management_change: Add this when dyspnea or heart failure features are present because elevated JVP changes congestion and right-heart strain assessment.
conditionalExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect and press lower extremity edema
conditionalExam.action: Add this when dyspnea or heart failure features are present because edema changes volume overload and venous thromboembolism framing.
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect and press over the lower legs or dependent areas for pitting, symmetry, tenderness, and extent.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Add when dyspnea, orthopnea, edema, heart failure, volume overload or related patient-specific modifiers are present in the selected Chest pain workup.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: volume overload, DVT signs, or asymmetric lower-extremity pathology
conditionalExam.management_change: Add this when dyspnea or heart failure features are present because edema changes volume overload and venous thromboembolism framing.
conditionalExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

conditionalExam.label: Palpate chest wall tenderness
conditionalExam.action: Add this when musculoskeletal features are present because reproducible tenderness changes nonischemic differential framing after red flags are addressed.
conditionalExam.technique: Palpate the chest wall over the area of pain, asking whether palpation reproduces the presenting discomfort.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Add when reproducible, movement, costochondritis, trauma or related patient-specific modifiers are present in the selected Chest pain workup.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: reproducible musculoskeletal chest wall tenderness after dangerous causes are considered
conditionalExam.management_change: Add this when musculoskeletal features are present because reproducible tenderness changes nonischemic differential framing after red flags are addressed.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect unilateral leg swelling
conditionalExam.action: Add this when PE or DVT concern is present because unilateral swelling changes venous thromboembolism probability and testing pathway.

options.label: Recent surgery, immobility, or long travel

conditionalExam.technique: Inspect both legs side by side for asymmetric swelling, erythema, collateral veins, tenderness, and size difference.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Add when leg swelling, dvt, pulmonary embolism, pleuritic, hypoxia or related patient-specific modifiers are present in the selected Chest pain workup.

conditionalExam.management_change: Add this when PE or DVT concern is present because unilateral swelling changes venous thromboembolism probability and testing pathway.

conditionalExam.label: Compare calf circumference
conditionalExam.action: Add this when PE or DVT concern is present because calf asymmetry changes venous thromboembolism probability and testing pathway.

options.label: Recent surgery, immobility, or long travel

conditionalExam.technique: Measure both calves at the same distance below the tibial tuberosity and compare circumference in centimeters.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Add when leg swelling, dvt, pulmonary embolism, pleuritic, hypoxia or related patient-specific modifiers are present in the selected Chest pain workup.

conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

conditionalExam.label: Compare radial pulse symmetry
conditionalExam.action: Add this when aortic syndrome concern is present because pulse asymmetry changes concern for acute aortic pathology.
conditionalExam.technique: Palpate bilateral pulses at the named sites, comparing amplitude, symmetry, rhythm, and perfusion.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Add when tearing, ripping, back, pulse deficit, aortic dissection or related patient-specific modifiers are present in the selected Chest pain workup.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: dehydration, shock physiology, or peripheral perfusion
conditionalExam.management_change: Add this when aortic syndrome concern is present because pulse asymmetry changes concern for acute aortic pathology.
